title: Add Description Field with Standard Object Lookup to Dictionary
story: As a DataTools Pro user, I want each object in my Data Dictionary to have a description field that is pre-populated with standard Salesforce object descriptions, so that I can quickly understand what each object represents and customize the description for my org's context.
description:
Adds a 'Description' field to the Data Dictionary scorecard. When a new object is added, the system looks up a table of standard Salesforce object descriptions to pre-populate the field. Users can then edit and customize the description.

For standard Salesforce objects (Account, Lead, Contact, etc.), the lookup table provides a default description explaining the object's purpose. For custom objects, the description field starts empty since the system cannot know the custom object's purpose.

The description appears in both the info window (object detail popup) and in the scorecard table.
acceptance_criteria: A 'Description' field appears on each object in the Data Dictionary
Standard Salesforce objects are pre-populated with descriptions from a lookup table
Custom objects have an empty description field
Users can edit the description field
Description is visible in the object info window
Description is visible in the scorecard table
Edits to the description persist after page reload
ux_changes: New 'Description' column in the scorecard table and the object info window. Editable like the existing Notes field. Shows pre-populated text for standard objects.
middleware_changes: Lookup logic checks a standard object description table when adding a new object. Pre-populates the description field if a match is found.
data_model_changes: New 'description' column on the scorecard/object table. New lookup table with standard Salesforce object descriptions.
implementation_notes: The lookup table of standard object descriptions helps clients identify common Salesforce objects. The description is written into the customer's dictionary record so they can freely edit and customize it. The field behaves like the existing Notes field.
comment_summary: Developer asked if the description field is similar to the existing Notes field. Product owner confirmed yes, and specified it should appear in both the info window and the table. Deployed to development.
